channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-335] SuSE 11.1 and SuSE server pack 1 support for Lustre 1.8 and 2.X
item.description: For Lustre 1.8.X and Lustre 2.X Suse support has been lagging. The goal is to bring this up to date on the server side.
item.summary: SuSE 11.1 and SuSE server pack 1 support for Lustre 1.8 and 2.X

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

Broke up the patch as Andreas suggested. First patch is for the miscellaneous build issues for any SuSE platform; http://review.whamcloud.com/#change,1678. The second patch is for SuSE SP1 support;
http://review.whamcloud.com/#change,575. Another patch SuSE SP0 can be sumbitted if anyone is interested.

Andreas I have looked at your suggestion of cleaning up enable-ext4. So I tested the build with disable-ext4 to see what happens and it exposed a lot of dependencies that have been hidden. For example in lustre/autoconf/lustre-core.m4 we have a test for ext4_discard_preacllocations which don't test for the enable-ext4 flag. So for this release perhaps we should just fail the build if ext4 is not available. What do you think?
Final push I hope to get merged into Lustre 2.1. Cray has reported positive feedback and has incorporated into their tree.

Updated the SuSE SP1 support to kernel 2.6.32.36-0.5. Please give it a try.

Lustre 2.0.63 branch

http://review.whamcloud.com/#change,575

Lustre 1.8.6 whamcloud branch:

http://review.whamcloud.com/#change,565

Lustre 2.0.62 branch

http://review.whamcloud.com/#change,575
James, can you please post a link to the master changeset?

Good news. I have SuSE port for SP0 and SP1 against the whamcloud branch. Same as the above changeset. If you follow the link you will see the notes against which kernel it builds against. The SuSE users in the community will have to agree upon a SuSE release that we all will use since supporting many kernels will be to difficult to do.

Okay I just pushed my SuSE changes for whamcloud b1_8. The whamcloud tree not in sync with the Oracle tree which now causes SP1 to not build on the whamcloud branch. My work does build SP1 on the oracle branch.Give it a shot.

http://review.whamcloud.com/#change,565
For 1.8.6 yes the work was to restore the SP0 support. I have a patch which I can make against whamclouds git for b1_8. Both SP0 and SP1 are supported at the same time.

James, SLES 11 SP1 support was completed in 1.8.5, however, it was done in a way that dropped SLES 11 base ("SP0") support. Are your 1.8.x changes focused on restoring broader SLES support?

I'd love to collaborate with you and Vladimir on the 2.1 patches.
